title: 10 Herbal Teas Proven to Cure Fungal Skin Infection
intro_desc: Herbal teas for fungal skin infection are a natural and effective way to treat fungal infections, such as athlete's foot, ringworm, and fungal acne, by providing antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat the infection. The best herbal teas for fungal skin infection include Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Artemisia absinthium, Ginkgo biloba, Aloe vera, Sanguinaria canadensis, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, fungal skin infections can lead to discomfort, pain, and embarrassment, affecting daily life, causing individuals to avoid social interactions and impact their self-esteem and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tea, also known as tea tree oil, is good for fungal skin infections. The tea tree oil has special properties that help kill fungi. It contains a compound called terpinen-4-ol, which is very good at killing fungi that cause infections. When you use Melaleuca alternifolia tea on your skin, it helps to reduce the number of fungi, which in turn helps to heal the infection. Tea tree oil is also good at reducing inflammation and redness, which can make the infection look worse. This can help to make the skin feel better and look healthier. The tea tree oil can also help to prevent the infection from spreading to other parts of the skin.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for fungal skin infection because it has antifungal properties that help stop the growth of fungus and reduce inflammation and pain on the affected skin.

Catechin is good for fungal skin infections because it has antimicrobial properties that help to kill fungi and prevent them from growing back, thereby promoting healthy skin and reducing the risk of infection.

Epigallocatechin gallate has been shown to have antifungal properties and may help to prevent the spread of fungal skin infections, such as ringworm and athlete's foot, by inhibiting the growth and reproduction of fungal cells.

Tannins, also known as oregano, are good for fungal skin infections because they have antifungal properties that can help kill the fungus and prevent it from growing, which can lead to a faster recovery and relief from symptoms.
Catechins, also known as oregano, have antifungal properties that can help to kill or stop the growth of fungus that causes skin infections.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with fungal skin infections, first you need to gather the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Origanum vulgare leaves, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Next, boil one liter of water and add two teaspoons of the dried leaves to it. Let it steep for five to seven minutes, then strain the liquid using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. Once the tea has cooled down, you can apply it to the affected skin area using a cotton ball or a cloth. You can also store the cooled tea in a bottle in the fridge for later use. It's essential to note that Origanum vulgare has antifungal properties that can help combat fungal skin infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm, but it's recommended to consult a doctor or a herbalist before using it as a treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea, also known as marigold tea, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to treat fungal skin infections. The tea is made from the flowers of the calendula plant, which contain powerful anti-inflammatory and antifungal compounds. These compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with fungal infections. They also inhibit the growth of fungal organisms, such as candida, that cause infections like athlete's foot and ringworm. Calendula officinalis tea has antiseptic properties that help to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing of the affected skin. When applied topically, the tea has been shown to soothe and calm the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting a healthy balance of skin flora. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce scarring and promote tissue repair.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea to help with fungal skin infections, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the Oregano plant, also known as Origanum vulgare. If using fresh leaves, wash them thoroughly with clean water and dry them with a paper towel. If using dried leaves, simply measure out one teaspoon of the dried leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water. Place the dried or fresh Oregano leaves in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup and pour in the boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds. Then, strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer and discard the solids. You can add honey to taste for sweetness, but it's best to consume the tea without any additives to reap its full benefits. Drinking 2-3 cups of the Origanum vulgare tea per day may help alleviate fungal skin infections, such as athlete's foot or ringworm, by its antimicrobial properties that combat fungal growth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea has been used for centuries to help treat fungal skin infections like athlete's foot and ringworm. This is because aloe vera has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help fight off the infection-causing fungi. The gel of the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to be effective in killing fungal cells and reducing inflammation. Drinking aloe vera tea can help to soothe and calm the skin from the inside out, reducing redness and itching. The tea also contains vitamins and minerals like vitamin C and E, which can help to boost the immune system and fight off the infection. Additionally, aloe vera tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort caused by fungal skin infections. This can help to promote healing and prevent scarring.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea, which helps with fungal skin infections, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried oregano leaves. If using fresh leaves, rinse them under cold water and pat dry with a paper towel. If using dried leaves, simply measure out one teaspoon for every 8 ounces of water. Next, boil 8 ounces of water in a saucepan and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add the oregano leaves to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the leaves, and then discard the solids. Allow the tea to cool before applying it to the affected skin area using a clean cotton ball. To make a more potent tea, you can also add a few drops of apple cider vinegar to the cooled tea, which is said to enhance the antifungal properties of oregano.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea has been used for a long time to help fight fungal skin infections. The main reason for this is that it has antifungal properties, which means it can kill or stop the growth of fungi that cause these infections. Echinacea purpurea tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the Echinacea purpurea plant, which contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and fight infection. When you drink Echinacea purpurea tea, the antifungal compounds are absorbed into your body and can help stop the spread of fungal infections on your skin. This can help to clear up symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling, and can even help to prevent the infection from coming back. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with fungal skin infections, start by gathering a handful of fresh oregano leaves or 2-3 teaspoons of dried leaves. Rinse the fresh leaves or dried leaves in a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, add the oregano leaves to a teapot or a heat-resistant cup and pour in 1 cup of boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh strainer into another cup to remove the oregano leaves. Your Origanum vulgare herbal tea is now ready to drink. To use it for treating fungal skin infections, you can soak a cotton pad in the tea and apply it directly to the affected area 2-3 times a day. You can also drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help your body fight off the infection from the inside. Note that you should consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, has been used for centuries to help treat various health issues, including fungal skin infections. The active ingredients in thyme, like thymol and carvacrol, have antifungal properties that can kill or prevent the growth of fungi. These compounds can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, making thyme tea a potential remedy for fungal skin infections like athlete's foot and ringworm. Thymol in particular, has been shown to be effective against a wide range of fungi, including those that cause skin infections. When consumed as a tea, thyme can be absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the skin, where it can help combat fungal infections from the inside out. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Origanum vulgare herbal tea that helps with fungal skin infections, start by gathering dried or fresh origanum vulgare leaves. If using fresh leaves, dry them in a low-heat oven or by air-drying them. Next, measure one teaspoon of dried leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves for every cup of boiling water. Pour the boiling water over the leaves in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup, then let it steep for five to seven minutes. Strain the tea into another cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the leaves. Once the tea has cooled, use it as a compress or apply it directly to the affected skin area by soaking a cotton pad in the tea and holding it in place with a bandage. Alternatively, you can drink the tea three to four times a day to help boost your immune system and fight fungal infections from the inside.

supplementary_best: A combination of 'Melaleuca alternifolia', 'Calendula officinalis', and 'Thymus vulgaris' can help remedy fungal skin infections. 'Melaleuca alternifolia', or tea tree oil, is known for its antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections. 'Calendula officinalis' or calendula, has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with fungal skin infections. 'Thymus vulgaris', or thyme, has antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause infections. This combination of herbs can help soothe and heal fungal skin infections by reducing inflammation, preventing bacterial and fungal growth, and promoting a healthy environment for skin healing.  This combination is effective because it addresses multiple aspects of fungal skin infections. Tea tree oil directly targets fungal infections, calendula reduces inflammation and promotes skin healing, and thyme prevents the growth of bacteria and fungi. This multi-faceted approach makes this combination of herbs a potentially effective remedy for fungal skin infections.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a fungal skin infection, it's best to avoid herbal teas that may make the condition worse. Tea tree oil and tea made from the leaves of the Melaleuca genus can be too strong and cause more irritation.  Some herbal teas to be careful with include those made from ginger, as high doses of ginger can make fungal infections worse.  Chamomile tea may seem like a safe choice, but it can be an allergen for some people, and a skin infection is an open wound, making it easier to get an allergic reaction.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help with fungal skin infections by providing a moist environment that inhibits the growth of fungi. The gel also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling. Tea tree oil, when applied topically, has antifungal properties that can kill fungal cells. It is often used in combination with other treatments to help clear up infections. Coconut oil contains lauric acid, which has antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections. It can be applied directly to the affected area to help soothe and calm the skin. Apple cider vinegar, when diluted with water and applied topically, can help balance the skin's pH and create an environment that is not favorable to fungal growth. Baking soda can also be used to create a paste that can be applied to the affected area, helping to dry out the skin and prevent fungal growth. The antifungal properties in these natural remedies can help alleviate symptoms and speed up the healing process, making them a useful addition to medical treatments.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with fungal skin infections often experience other health issues due to the weakened immune system and inflammation. They may get frequent respiratory infections like bronchitis or pneumonia because their body's natural defenses are down. Echinacea and garlic have antiviral properties that can help boost the immune system and fight off these infections.  They may also experience digestive problems like bloating, diarrhea, or stomach pain due to impaired gut health. Probiotics and slippery elm can help soothe the gut and promote a healthy balance of bacteria, reducing inflammation and discomfort.  Additionally, fungal skin infections can lead to nutrient deficiencies, particularly in vitamin B12, iron, and zinc, which are essential for skin health. Taking supplements like vitamin B12 and iron may be necessary, but herbal remedies like nettle and alfalfa can also help replenish these nutrients and support skin health.  Some people may experience skin sensitivity, redness, and irritation due to the fungal infection. Aloe vera and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can calm the skin and reduce these symptoms.  Lastly, people with fungal skin infections may experience fatigue and lethargy due to the body's increased energy expenditure fighting off the infection. Ginseng and ashwagandha can help reduce stress and boost energy levels, making it easier to manage the symptoms of the fungal infection.
